title: Sarcotheca glabra
intro: Sarcotheca glabra, also known as the hairy sarcotheca, is a species of flowering plant in the family Vitaceae. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Sarcotheca glabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat skin conditions, fever, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Sarcotheca glabra is a species of flowering plant in the family Vitaceae, commonly known as the grape family. It belongs to the genus Sarcotheca, which is a group of climbing plants native to tropical Asia. The classification of Sarcotheca glabra is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Vitales, family Vitaceae, genus Sarcotheca, species S. glabra. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other organisms. Sarcotheca glabra is a member of the Vitaceae family, which is characterized by its grape-like fruits and climbing habit.
